extraction.one_line_summary: TEUNS Dylan stole victory from DENNIS Rowan on steep finish climb to Romont after DENNIS went from 2km out using TT power
tactical_patterns: INEOS deployed DEPLUS for extended pacemaking on stage one despite having prologue leader, revealing domestique depth over GC protection.
DENNIS launched solo attack 2km from steep finish using time trial power rather than waiting for sprint group.
Break dissolution at 30km triggered CAVANIA solo counter at 5km, suggesting Quick-Step testing without full commitment.
HAYTER crash at 14km forced INEOS to abandon pace control, opening tactical opportunities for GC rivals.

rider_signals.DENNIS Rowan: positive: time trial engines can successfully attack steep uphill finishes from distance, not just flat rouleur terrain
rider_signals.DEPLUS Laurens: positive: showed exceptional form doing sustained pacemaking work, signaling strong early-season condition for INEOS
rider_signals.TEUNS Dylan: positive: demonstrated ability to close significant gap and outsprint stronger time trialist on steep gradient
rider_signals.MCNULTY Brandon: neutral: initiated final climb attack but lacked punch to hold off stronger closers
rider_signals.HAYTER Ethan: negative: lost race lead to crash at 14km, suggesting positioning vulnerability in hilly parcours
